summary: The final implementation correctly addresses CWE-78 (OS Command Injection) and CWE-352 (Cross-Site Request Forgery) by validating commands against a whitelist, using shell=False in subprocess, and enforcing CSRF token matching. The only SAST finding (bandit:B603) was correctly triaged as a false positive by the judge.
suggested_actions: Ensure the session CSRF token storage is properly initialized (e.g., on login) to avoid runtime errors in validate_csrf_token
Add error handling for missing form fields (command, csrf_token) to prevent KeyError
Consider using a more robust session management mechanism instead of just checking cookie prefix
